title: Modules, Packages, and Dependency Boundaries · Async Failure Triage
summary: Split the status coordinator into explicit browser modules and verify clean-environment loading and dependency behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a modular status application with a dependency record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.
Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
Use dynamic import for conditional capability or measured loading needs with complete error states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
